parameters.content:
## INGESTION

Signal polling (every 5s) + Webhook entry point.
Both paths normalize to unified message format.

Schedule Trigger > Poll Signal > Filter Empty > Split > Normalize
Webhook Trigger > Normalize

## COMMAND PARSER

Extracts flags (quick, deep, help, etc.) and URL from message.
Routes help requests to Help Response.

## IDENTITY & CONTENT KEY

Resolves sender identity via pipeline_users (UPSERT).
Normalizes URL to content key for cache lookup.

## CACHE CHECK & ROUTING

Checks content_cache + content_variants for existing results.
Cache hit: serve immediately. Cache miss: queue job.

## ERROR HANDLING

Catches pipeline failures, formats user-friendly error message,
sends back via Signal.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes:
Poll interval: 5 seconds.

WHAT: Triggers Signal message polling on a fixed schedule.
WHY: signal-cli-rest-api does not support push webhooks.
HOW: Fires every 5s, triggers Poll Signal API.
NEXT: Poll Signal API fetches pending messages.

nodes.name: Poll Signal API
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Fetches pending messages from signal-cli REST API.
WHY: Retrieves messages that arrived since last poll.
HOW: GET /v1/receive/+NUMBER returns array of message envelopes.
NEXT: Filter Empty skips processing when no messages.

nodes.name: Filter Empty
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Skips processing when Signal API returns empty array.
WHY: Most polls return nothing -- avoid running pipeline on empty data.
HOW: Checks if response data != '[]'.
NEXT: True branch goes to Split Messages.

nodes.name: Split Messages
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Processes Signal messages one at a time.
WHY: Each message may be a different content type/command.
HOW: Splits array into individual items.
NEXT: Each item goes to Normalize Input.

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Receives direct HTTP POST requests (bookmarklet, shortcuts).
WHY: Desktop entry point via browser bookmarklet.
HOW: Listens at /webhook/signal-pipeline. Expects JSON: {url, flags, source}.
NEXT: Normalize Input merges with Signal format.

nodes.name: Normalize Input
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Merges Signal and webhook inputs into unified format.
WHY: All downstream nodes work with one consistent shape.
HOW: Detects source, extracts message text, sender, attachment info.
NEXT: Command Parser extracts flags and URL from _message.

nodes.name: Command Parser
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Extracts command flags and URL from message text.
WHY: Flags control pipeline behavior (quick, deep, help, etc.).
HOW: Regex matching + text removal per PRD spec.
NEXT: Help Check routes help; main pipeline continues if not help.

nodes.name: Help Check
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Routes help requests to help response.
HOW: Checks _flags.help boolean.
NEXT: True -> Help Response. False -> Resolve Identity.

nodes.name: Send Help
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Sends help text back to the sender via Signal API.
WHY: User requested help command.
HOW: POST to Signal v2/send with dynamic recipient UUID.

parameters.query: INSERT INTO pipeline_users (name, role, signal_uuid) VALUES ('Unknown', 'user', '{{ $('Command Parser').first().json._senderUuid }}') ON CONFLICT (signal_uuid) DO UPDATE SET name = pipeline_users.name RETURNING id, name, role;

nodes.name: Resolve Identity
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Looks up or creates the sender in pipeline_users.
WHY: Every message needs a resolved user identity for role-based routing.
HOW: UPSERT -- inserts new user as role='user' if not found, returns existing if found.
     Uses ON CONFLICT (signal_uuid) DO UPDATE to always return a row.
NEXT: Normalize Content Key extracts cache key from URL.

nodes.name: Normalize Content Key
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Extracts a stable content key from the URL for cache lookups.
WHY: Same content shared by different users should hit the same cache entry.
HOW: YouTube -> 'yt:<video_id>'. Web -> 'web:<cleaned_url>' (tracking params stripped).
     Also attaches resolved identity (_userId, _userRole, _userName).
NEXT: Cache Check queries PostgreSQL for existing results.

nodes.name: Has URL?
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Checks if the message contains a URL.
WHY: Messages without URLs (braindumps, text) don't need cache lookup or queuing.
HOW: Checks _url is not null/empty.
NEXT: True -> Cache Check. False -> dropped (no action for text-only in this workflow).

parameters.query: SELECT cc.content_key, cc.content_type, cc.title, cc.source_url, cc.transcript_path, cc.vault_path, cc.processed_at, cc.processed_by, cv.result AS _cachedSummary, cv.variant_type AS _cachedVariant FROM content_cache cc LEFT JOIN content_variants cv ON cc.content_key = cv.content_key AND cv.variant_type = 'summary' WHERE cc.content_key = '{{ $('Normalize Content Key').first().json._contentKey }}' LIMIT 1;

nodes.name: Cache Check
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Queries content_cache + content_variants for existing processed results.
WHY: Avoid re-processing content that was already summarized.
HOW: JOINs cache with summary variant on content_key.
NEXT: Has Cache? routes to serve cached result or queue new job.

nodes.name: Check Cache Result
nodes.notes: Checks if Cache Check returned a cached row. Sets _hasCacheHit flag.

nodes.name: Serve Cached
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Formats cached summary for Signal response.
WHY: Avoid re-processing -- serve from cache immediately.
HOW: Admin gets truncated summary + Forgejo link. User gets full text.
     If admin and no vault_path, flags for vault write job.
NEXT: Send Cache Response delivers via Signal API.

nodes.name: Send Cache Response
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Sends cached result back to sender via Signal API.
WHY: Deliver the cached summary immediately.
HOW: POST to Signal v2/send with dynamic recipient UUID.

nodes.name: Queue Job
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Inserts a new processing job into pipeline_queue.
WHY: Content not in cache -- needs to be processed by the Processing workflow.
HOW: Admin jobs get priority='immediate'. User jobs get priority='scheduled' (next 4 AM).
NEXT: Send Ack notifies the sender that their request is queued.

nodes.name: Send Ack
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Builds acknowledgment message for the sender.
WHY: User should know their request was received and when to expect results.
HOW: Admin sees 'Processing...' + job ID. User sees 'Queued for 4 AM'.
NEXT: Send Ack Message delivers via Signal API.

nodes.name: Send Ack Message
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Sends queue acknowledgment back to sender via Signal API.
WHY: Immediate feedback that the request was received.
HOW: POST to Signal v2/send with dynamic recipient UUID.

nodes.name: Error Handler
nodes.notes:
WHAT: Catches pipeline failures, formats user-friendly error.
WHY: User should get informative feedback, not raw stack traces.
HOW: Extracts error message, adds troubleshooting tips.
NEXT: Send Error Msg sends error back via Signal.

nodes.name: Send Error Msg
nodes.notes: WHAT: Sends error message back to user via Signal.
